revision.comment: create
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kinpō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinpō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinpō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinpō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinpō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinpō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}. The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinpō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.kinbou.net/ Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Tsuruoka, Yamagata]] {{Shinto-stub}}

It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.kinbou.net/ Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Tsuruoka, Yamagata]] {{Shinto-stub}}

A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.kinbou.net/ Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Tsuruoka, Yamagata]] {{Shinto-stub}}

Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.kinbou.net/ Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Tsuruoka, Yamagata]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] {{Shinto-stub}}

A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Tsuruoka, Yamagata]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] {{Shinto-stub}}

It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Tsuruoka, Yamagata]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] {{Shinto-stub}}

It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Tsuruoka, Yamagata]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:Mountain faith]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia01]] {{Shinto-stub}}

A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Tsuruoka, Yamagata]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:Mountain faith]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia29]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia29]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia01]] {{Shinto-stub}}

The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Tsuruoka, Yamagata]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:Mountain faith]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia29]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia29]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kinbō revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages and 金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} [[category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages and 金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages and {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages and {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages and {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages and Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages and revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38|40|43.87|N|139|48|19.99|E|type:landmark_region:JP-06_scale:25000|display=inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[Tsuruoka, Yamagata|Tsuruoka]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[Hiraizumi]] during the late [[Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages and
