The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[Northern Fujiwara]] of [[:en:Hiraizumi|Hiraizumi]] during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[Shōnai Domain]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] }} [[sn:Kinbō Shrine]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[:en:Japanese Zen|Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] }} [[sn:Kinbō Shrine]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[:en:Japanese Zen|Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] }} [[sn:Kinbō Shrine]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[:en:Japanese Zen|Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] }} [[sn:Kinbō Shrine]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[:en:Jōryaku|Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[:en:Northern Fujiwara|Northern Fujiwara]] of [[:en:Hiraizumi|Hiraizumi]] during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[:en:Shōnai Domain|Shōnai Domain]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[:en:Japanese Zen|Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] }} [[sn:Kinbō Shrine]] [[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q11647032}}

The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[:en:Japanese Zen|Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] }} [[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11647032}}

In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. 金峯神社 | image = File:YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.678853|N|139.805553|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} | location = {{ill|Tsuruoka|WD=Q734416}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[:en:Jōryaku|Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[:en:Northern Fujiwara|Northern Fujiwara]] of [[:en:Hiraizumi|Hiraizumi]] during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[:en:Shōnai Domain|Shōnai Domain]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[:en:Japanese Zen|Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] }} [[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11647032}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Kinpō Jinja]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] [[commons:Category:Kinpō Jinja]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kinpō Jinja]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647032]] Wikidata name: Kinpō Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647032]] Wikidata name: Kinpō Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = File:YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.678853|N|139.805553|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} | location = {{ill|Tsuruoka|WD=Q734416}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[:en:Jōryaku|Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[:en:Northern Fujiwara|Northern Fujiwara]] of [[:en:Hiraizumi|Hiraizumi]] during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[:en:Shōnai Domain|Shōnai Domain]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[:en:Japanese Zen|Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11647032}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kinpō Jinja]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kinpō Jinja]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kinpō Jinja]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647032]] Wikidata name: Kinpō Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647032]] Wikidata name: Kinpō Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = File:YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.678853|N|139.805553|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} | location = {{ill|Tsuruoka|WD=Q734416}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the [[:en:Daidō|Daidō]] era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the [[:en:Jōryaku|Jōryaku]] era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in [[:en:Yoshino, Nara|Yoshino]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. It was a place of worship for the [[:en:Northern Fujiwara|Northern Fujiwara]] of [[:en:Hiraizumi|Hiraizumi]] during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by [[:en:Shōnai Domain|Shōnai Domain]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at [[:en:Japanese Zen|Japanese Zen]] temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11647032}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It was a place of worship for the {{ill|Northern Fujiwara|en|Northern Fujiwara|WD=Q1050942|lt=Northern Fujiwara}} of {{ill|Hiraizumi|en|Hiraizumi|WD=Q1203004|lt=Hiraizumi}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain}} during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}.This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at {{ill|Japanese Zen|en|Japanese Zen|WD=Q189424|lt=Japanese Zen}} temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11647032}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It was a place of worship for the {{ill|Northern Fujiwara|en|Northern Fujiwara|WD=Q1050942|lt=Northern Fujiwara}} of {{ill|Hiraizumi|en|Hiraizumi|WD=Q1203004|lt=Hiraizumi}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain}} during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}.This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at {{ill|Japanese Zen|en|Japanese Zen|WD=Q189424|lt=Japanese Zen}} temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11647032}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kinpō Jinja]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kinpō Jinja]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kinpō Jinja]][[en:Kinbō Shrine]][[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kinbō Jinja
金峯神社 | image = File:YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kinbojinja-01.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Kinbō Jinja | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.678853|N|139.805553|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Kinbō Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Sukunabikona]], [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Emperor Ankan]] | founded_by = | established = early-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} | date_destroyed = | location = Tsuruoka, Yamagata | website = {{URL|http://www.kinbou.net/ }} | architecture_style = | festival = June 15 | leadership = | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=green|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]]}} | location = {{ill|Tsuruoka|WD=Q734416}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kinbō Shrine'''|金峯神社 |Kinbō Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Tsuruoka|en|Tsuruoka|WD=Q734416|lt=Tsuruoka}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. A former prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], the entire summit of Mount Kinbō behind the shrine is regarded as its ''[[honden]]''. The mountain is designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Place of Scenic Beauty]].{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/297 |title=金峯山|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} The shrine's main festival is held annually on June 15. ==History== The original construction of this shrine is unknown, but it is said by shrine records to have been established in the {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} era (806-810 AD), although other shrine records indicate that it was established in the {{ill|Jōryaku|en|Jōryaku|WD=Q279743|lt=Jōryaku}} era (990-995 AD) as a subsidiary of the Kinpusen Jinja in {{ill|Yoshino, Nara|en|Yoshino, Nara|WD=Q1203731|lt=Yoshino}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}. It was a place of worship for the {{ill|Northern Fujiwara|en|Northern Fujiwara|WD=Q1050942|lt=Northern Fujiwara}} of {{ill|Hiraizumi|en|Hiraizumi|WD=Q1203004|lt=Hiraizumi}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain}} during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}.This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at {{ill|Japanese Zen|en|Japanese Zen|WD=Q189424|lt=Japanese Zen}} temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11647032}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

It was a place of worship for the {{ill|Northern Fujiwara|en|Northern Fujiwara|WD=Q1050942|lt=Northern Fujiwara|1=Northern Fujiwara}} of {{ill|Hiraizumi|en|Hiraizumi|WD=Q1203004|lt=Hiraizumi|1=Hiraizumi}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Under the ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō]]'' of the pre-modern era, it was regarded as a [[Shingon]] temple and was a training center for the ''[[Shugendō]] [[Yamabushi]]'', and was supported by {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain|1=Shōnai Domain}} during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at {{ill|Japanese Zen|en|Japanese Zen|WD=Q189424|lt=Japanese Zen|1=Japanese Zen}} temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Kinbō Shrine]] [[ja:金峯神社 (鶴岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11647032}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

In 1870, after the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], it became a "Mitake Shrine". It was renamed Kinbō Jinja in 1877. The shrine's [[Heiden (Shinto)|''Heiden'']] has been designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.This structure has an inscription on one of its beams indicating that it was remodeled by [[Mogami Yoshimitsu]] in 1608, indicating that it pre-dates the start of the Edo period. It has a unique style, similar to that of a chapel found at {{ill|Japanese Zen|en|Japanese Zen|qid=Q189424|lt=Japanese Zen|1=Japanese Zen}} temples with a high roof and Chinese-style gable over its entrance.{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/124717|title=金峯神社本殿 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Yamagata)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{wikidata link|Q11647032|co|commons:Category:Kinpō Jinja|en|Kinbō Shrine|ja|金峯神社 (鶴岡市)}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Places of Scenic Beauty]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
